title: Pluchea rubelliflora
intro: Pluchea rubelliflora, also known as the red-flowered alkanna, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pluchea rubelliflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pluchea rubelliflora is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, as well as being used in ointments and creams for topical applications.
taxonomy: Pluchea rubelliflora is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification places it in the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. At the rank of order, Pluchea rubelliflora is also classified as an angiosperm, a flowering plant. The classification can be summarized as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, family Asteraceae.
uses: Pluchea rubelliflora is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research has shown that extracts from Pluchea rubelliflora may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. benefits: Pluchea rubelliflora is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
